highest_risk_counterexamples.message: queue tenant-index-fallback-scan replay count=18000 lacks dedupe_key, idempotency proof, or dedupe window
highest_risk_counterexamples.remediation: Add a dedupe_key, prove the handler idempotent, or model a positive dedupe_window_minutes before replay.
highest_risk_counterexamples.runbook: Grafana Tempo current public runbook derived safety model

highest_risk_counterexamples.message: queue tenant-index-fallback-scan has replayed messages without modeled deduplication
highest_risk_counterexamples.remediation: Stop replay or add deduplication/idempotency guards before messages can be processed twice.
highest_risk_counterexamples.runbook: Grafana Tempo current public runbook derived safety model

highest_risk_counterexamples.message: queue tenant-index-fallback-scan has depth=36000 before rebalance to 0 consumers
highest_risk_counterexamples.remediation: Rebalance to a positive consumer count while backlog exists, or drain the queue first.
highest_risk_counterexamples.runbook: Grafana Tempo current public runbook derived safety model

highest_risk_counterexamples.message: queue tenant-index-fallback-scan has depth=36000 with no active consumers
highest_risk_counterexamples.remediation: Keep at least one active consumer or drain backlog before rebalancing to zero consumers.
highest_risk_counterexamples.runbook: Grafana Tempo current public runbook derived safety model

highest_risk_counterexamples.message: queue tenant-index-fallback-scan has depth=36000 while consumer group rebalance is not stable
highest_risk_counterexamples.remediation: Wait for consumer-group stability before leaving replay/backlog processing exposed.
highest_risk_counterexamples.runbook: Grafana Tempo current public runbook derived safety model

unverified_prose_claims.excerpt: > If you can isolate the impacted tenants, attempt to take targeted action instead of making sweeping changes. Your easiest lever to pull is to simply delete stale tenant indexes as all components will fallback to bucket listing.
unverified_prose_claims.message: Prose describes data deletion without executable restore/blast-radius preconditions. Missing condition(s): service_available_at_least.
unverified_prose_claims.recommendation: Require a targeted scope, backup/restore validation, and capacity guard before destructive data operations.

unverified_prose_claims.excerpt: > If you can isolate the impacted tenants, attempt to take targeted action instead of making sweeping changes. Your easiest lever to pull is to simply delete stale tenant indexes as all components will fallback to bucket listing.
unverified_prose_claims.message: Prose describes destructive removal/forgetting without executable blast-radius checks. Missing condition(s): service_available_at_least.
unverified_prose_claims.recommendation: Model the removal as an action guarded by targeted scope, capacity, and rollback/restore preconditions.

unverified_prose_claims.excerpt: machine-checkable guardrails for deduplicated fallback replay, consumer-group

unverified_prose_claims.recommendation: Add queue_depth_at_most, queue_has_consumers, and deduplicated replay preconditions, or document an explicit limitation.
